title: Improve proposal execution UX: run-in-chat default, visibility, and command reliability
summary: Make approved proposals execute in the approving chat by default, add first-class proposal-to-task visibility, and enforce reliable validation command execution with real exit-code checks.
details:
## Problem
Users are seeing approved proposals execute in the background without clear in-chat visibility, and validation commands appear to complete even when launched detached (`cmd /k`), which can produce misleading success states.

## Goals
1. Approved proposals should execute in the same chat/session that approved them by default.
2. If execution is backgrounded, users must get a visible, clickable task linkage and status trail.
3. Validation/build/test steps must not use detached shells; completion must be tied to real process exit codes.

## Implementation Plan

### A) Proposal execution routing (default: same chat)
- Update proposal approval handling to capture `approvingSessionId` / `approvingChannel` and prefer it as execution target.
- Add explicit fallback order:
  1. approving chat/session
  2. proposal origin chat/session
  3. default agent session
- Add a user-facing execution mode field in proposal metadata:
  - `execution_mode: "in_chat" | "background"`
  - default `in_chat` when approved from chat UI.

### B) Proposal ↔ task observability
- Persist `task_id` on proposal record immediately when execution starts.
- In chat approval response, always post:
  - task id
  - current status
  - quick actions hints (view/get/resume)
- In proposals panel, add status badge and direct link to running task logs.
- Ensure terminal/log stream includes both proposal id and task id in each run header.

### C) Command execution reliability (no detached cmd)
- Replace detached patterns like:
  - `start cmd /k "npm run build"`
  - `start cmd /k "npm test"`
- Enforce direct foreground command invocation through the command runner so completion depends on process exit.
- Add guardrail in runner:
  - if command string matches detached shell patterns (`start cmd`, `cmd /k`, similar), mark as invalid for validation steps and return actionable error.
- Ensure run report includes:
  - exit code
  - stdout/stderr capture summary
  - pass/fail per validation step

### D) Completion semantics
- Do not mark step/task complete until validation commands return successful exits.
- If command is still running or detached, status must remain `running` or `failed` with reason.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Approving a proposal in a chat shows execution progress in that same chat by default.
- Every approved proposal has a visible linked `task_id` and can be inspected from both chat and proposals panel.
- Validation commands use non-detached execution only; task completion is blocked on real exit codes.
- Logs clearly explain why any command was rejected (e.g., detached shell pattern).

## Risk/Compatibility
- Low-to-medium risk; mostly routing/UX and command policy hardening.
- Background execution still supported when explicitly selected.

## Validation Plan
- Approve test proposal in-chat and confirm in-chat step streaming.
- Approve test proposal from panel and verify chosen execution mode behavior.
- Run build/test with both valid and detached commands; confirm detached is rejected and valid commands report real exits.

affectedFiles.description: Route execution to approving chat/session by default and set execution mode metadata
Persist task_id linkage and execution metadata
Show linked task status and quick navigation to logs
Ensure proposal/task correlation IDs are present in run headers and status events
Block detached shell patterns for validation and enforce exit-code-gated completion
Prevent step completion until validation command exits successfully

estimatedImpact: Higher trust in proposal execution, immediate user visibility of running work, and elimination of false-positive validation completions from detached command launches.
executorPrompt: Implement proposal execution UX and reliability improvements exactly as specified in this proposal: (1) default approved proposals to execute in approving chat/session, (2) persist and surface proposal↔task linkage in chat and proposals UI, (3) block detached cmd patterns for validation and gate completion on real exit codes, and (4) ensure step/task completion semantics reflect true process status. After edits, run build/tests with non-detached commands only and report changed files plus validation results.
